title: JetBlue is cutting a route between Boston and this southern city
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue is discontinuing the seasonal Boston to Asheville, North Carolina route due to unmet performance expectations.
The Boston-Asheville route, launched in 2022, was part of JetBlue's 2021 schedule expansion.
Customers affected by the route cancellation will receive a full refund, according to JetBlue's statement.

title: Theater review: ‘Stereophonic’ at Emerson Colonial really makes you feel like you’re with the band
author: Jackie Chianca
bullets: "Stereophonic" runs at Emerson Colonial Theatre in Boston, featuring a cast led by Denver Milord and Claire DeJean.
The play captures the tension and complexity of a band's recording experience in a 1976 Sausalito studio setting.
"Stereophonic" includes original compositions by Grammy Award-winning musician Will Butler of Arcade Fire.

title: Joe Mazzulla referenced his high school principal while defending Jaylen Brown after ejection
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected during the Celtics' 125-116 loss to the Spurs after receiving two technical fouls.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla defended Brown's ejection by referencing his high school principal's decisive actions.
Mazzulla expressed full support for Brown, stating he understood Brown's frustrations with the officiating.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest movement announces March rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The 'No Kings' protest rally is set for March 28 from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m. at Boston Common.
The movement, opposing authoritarianism, is organized by ACLU Massachusetts, Indivisible Mass Coalition, and Mass 50501.
Organizers expect the rally to be the largest yet, emphasizing democracy and resistance against Trump administration policies.

title: Now’s the time to book that Cape Cod getaway
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Cape Cod summer rentals are already 65% occupied for July and August, experts advise booking soon.
Chatham Bars Inn on Cape Cod received a five-star rating from Forbes Travel Guide in 2026.
Conde Nast Traveler named Cape Cod a top Memorial Day weekend destination for travelers.

title: Charlie McAvoy’s reward as Bruins’ OT hero? Another trip to the dentist’s chair.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the overtime winner for the Bruins in a 2-1 victory over the Los Angeles Kings.
Despite losing teeth and requiring dental work, McAvoy returned to play after a second-period injury in Tuesday's game.
The Bruins have won 13 consecutive home games, now just a point behind Detroit for the top wild-card spot in the East.

title: After her son was struck by a car, she’s running the Boston Marathon for Newton firefighters
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Maureen Murphy is running in the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Newton Firefighters Children's Fund.
Murphy's son, Teddy, was struck by a car two years ago, and NFD provided emergency care.
Proceeds raised will aid children of fallen firefighters across the Commonwealth through the NFD non-profit.

title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points, including 8 three-pointers, in Spurs' 125-116 win over the Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was ejected for persistent complaints against officiating after scoring eight points in the game.
Derrick White led the Celtics with 34 points, showcasing strong defense and offensive creativity against the Spurs.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Colonial home in Abington sold for $555,000; it was built in 1900 and has three bedrooms.
In Andover, a contemporary house on Hazelwood Circle sold for $1,778,500; it features five bedrooms.
A row-middle property on Chestnut St. in Boston sold for $22,000,000; it includes six bedrooms and nine baths.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the winning goal 39 seconds into overtime, leading the Bruins to a 2-1 victory over the Kings.
McAvoy played through facial injuries, having been hit in the mouth during a previous game and required further dental work.
Despite his injury setbacks, McAvoy is praised for his resilience and leadership potential within the Bruins team.
